chunks.text: NI LabVIEW Software - UCB Site License NI LabVIEW Software - UCB Site License public page Description: LabVIEW is a graphical programming environment from National Instruments that is designed to help engineers and scientists visualize, create, an

elp engineers and scientists visualize, create, and code engineering systems and ensure the quality of connected devices. It is widely used in engineering, physics and chemistry. MULTISIM is graphical circuit design software from National Instr

phical circuit design software from National Instruments that includes the SPICE simulation environment and produces printed circuit board (PCB) designs. LabVIEW Licensing The Colleges of Engineering and of Letters & Sciences (Physics and th

ering and of Letters & Sciences (Physics and the Space Sciences Lab) are sponsoring licenses for UC Berkeley faculty, staff and students on both university-owned and personally-owned computers. There is currently no charge to those indiv

s. There is currently no charge to those individuals for licenses. This WEB site is for the licenses only. Users should seek technical support from their own desktop support staff or from the National Instruments WEB site. This WEB site can a

National Instruments WEB site. This WEB site can also be accessed from https://software.berkeley.edu/labview . License Categories and Types Students may download versions for Windows, Macs and Linux and a 1-year license key. Faculty, Researchers and

nd a 1-year license key. Faculty, Researchers and Staff may download versions for Windows, Macs and Linux and a license key. Software included with the University of California - Berkeley Academic Site License : http://www.ni.com/white-paper/8115/en/

e License : http://www.ni.com/white-paper/8115/en/ LabVIEW and NI hardware technical support: With UC Berkeley's LabVIEW license, all faculty, researchers, and students have access to NI's Application Engineering support team. All users are en

ion Engineering support team. All users are encouraged to leverage the LabVIEW training resources (see "Training options" section below) and vast support resources included in LabVIEW and on ni.com before reaching out to NI's technical s

ni.com before reaching out to NI's technical support engineers, but that is included if immediate technical assistance is needed. NI Technical Support (Create an NI account with your @berkeley.edu address, and open a support case by verifying

edu address, and open a support case by verifying with the NI Service ID ) LabVIEW training options: For new LabVIEW or NI hardware users: ni.com/lv101 For more advanced/ thorough self-paced LabVIEW training: (10+ courses available): http://us.ni.com

raining: (10+ courses available): http://us.ni.com/academic/training (Click "Access Online Training", log in with UC email) Vendor: National Instuments Categories: Scientific, Engineering, Physics, Space Sciences, Chemistry Available Platforms: Windo
